function.name: code_run
function.description: Code executor. Prefer python. No concurrent calls. Put code in ```python/powershell blocks in reply body to avoid escaping. No hardcoding bulk data

script.description: [Optional] NEVER use this param when code is in ```python/powershell blocks in reply body. Only use when no code block in reply. Mutually exclusive with reply code blocks

function.name: file_read
function.description: Read file content. Read before modifying to get latest context and line numbers. Supports pagination and keyword search

keyword.description: Optional search keyword. If provided, returns first match (case-insensitive) with surrounding context

show_linenos.description: Show line numbers. Recommended on to help file_patch locate content

function.name: file_patch
function.description: Fine-grained local file edit. Finds unique old_content block and replaces with new_content. old_content must be unique in file with exact whitespace/indentation/newlines. On match failure, use file_read to re-check file content

old_content.description: Original text block to replace (must be unique in file)

new_content.description: New text content. Supports {{file:path:startLine:endLine}} syntax to reference file lines, auto-expanded before writing

function.name: file_write
function.description: Create, overwrite or append files. Use file_patch for fine-grained edits. Content must be in <file_content> tags or code blocks in reply body. Supports {{file:path:startLine:endLine}} to reference file lines, auto-expanded before writing

function.name: web_scan
function.description: Get simplified HTML and tab list of current page. Simplification filters sidebars/floating elements; use execute_js for filtered content. Call after switching pages

tabs_only.description: Return only tab list and current tab info, no HTML

switch_tab_id.description: Optional tab ID. If provided, switches to that tab before scanning

function.name: web_execute_js
function.description: Execute JS to control browser. Use precisely to reduce web_scan calls. Put code in ```javascript blocks in reply body to avoid escaping

script.description: [Optional] JS code or file path. NEVER use this param when code is in ```javascript blocks in reply body. Only use when no code block in reply. Mutually exclusive with reply code blocks

save_to_file.description: Save result to file, for long return values

no_monitor.description: Skip page change monitoring, saves 2-3s. Only set for pure reads, not for page actions

function.name: update_working_checkpoint
function.description:
Short-term working notepad, auto-injected each turn to prevent info loss in long tasks. Call during early/mid stages, not at end. When: (1) after reading SOP, store user needs & key constraints (skip for simple 1-2 step tasks); (2) before subtask switch or context flush; (3) after repeated failures, re-read SOP and must store new findings; (4) on new task, update content, clear old progress but keep valid constraints.

Don't call: simple tasks (1-2 steps), task completed (use long-term memory tool)

key_info.description: Replaces current notepad (<200 tokens). Incremental update: review existing, keep valid, add/remove/modify. Store: pitfalls, user requirements, key params/findings, file paths, progress, next steps. Don't store: ephemeral info, obvious context, old task info when user switched tasks. Prefer over-updating over losing key info

related_sop.description: Related SOP name(s), re-read when needed

function.name: ask_user
function.description: Interrupt task to ask user when needing decisions, extra info, or facing unresolvable blockers

candidates.description: Optional quick-select choices for the user

function.name: start_long_term_update
function.description: Start distilling long-term memory. Call when discovering info worth remembering (env facts/user prefs/lessons learned). Skip if memory already updated or in autonomous flow. Must call for tasks taking 15+ turns
